remember: Students will be able to recall the main features of the revenue system in pre-colonial and colonial India.
Students will be able to identify the key characteristics of the peasant, zamindar, and state in different periods.
Students will be able to list the main sources of revenue for the state in pre-colonial and colonial India.
understand: Students will be able to explain the role of the zamindar in the revenue system during the pre-colonial period.
Students will be able to describe the changes brought about by the Permanent Settlement of 1793.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of the Permanent Settlement on the peasants and zamindars.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of the revenue system to a specific historical scenario.
Students will be able to use the characteristics of the peasant and zamindar to understand their experiences in different periods.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the state, peasant, and zamindar in pre-colonial and colonial India.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of the revenue system on the Indian economy and society.

ncert_objectives: To understand the main features of the revenue system in pre-colonial and colonial India.
To analyze the role of the zamindar in the revenue system during the pre-colonial period.
To evaluate the impact of the Permanent Settlement of 1793 on the peasants and zamindars.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the key features of the revenue system in pre-colonial and colonial India.
To describe the changes brought about by the Permanent Settlement of 1793.
To explain the impact of the revenue system on the Indian economy and society.
